title: CI Bootstrap Smoke
objective: Extend CI so every change proves that `init-docs.sh` can initialize a fresh target repo on the supported OS matrix.
description: The current CI only runs `shellcheck` and parses a couple of JSON files. It does not exercise the actual bootstrap path. This slice adds a generated-project smoke test so regressions in initialization are caught before a release reaches adopters.
must: Extend the GitHub Actions workflow with a smoke test that creates a temporary target repo and runs `init-docs.sh` end to end
Run the smoke test on the supported OS matrix for Quiver bootstrap compatibility
Assert the generated project has the expected scripts, docs, specs, and workflow assets after initialization
not_included: Fixture coverage for every gate script
acceptance: CI fails if `init-docs.sh` breaks on either supported operating system
The smoke test asserts that the generated project contains the expected baseline assets and npm scripts
The smoke test can be run locally by a maintainer without relying on GitHub-only features
tests: GitHub Actions OS matrix bootstrap smoke test
Local smoke test for generated project initialization
assumptions: The supported bootstrap matrix is macOS and Linux
CI can create temporary git repos during workflow execution
